commands.author: 0xghost42
commands.command: acknowledged on the real-environment gap — wanted to be transparent about why the pr body's proof stops where it does. this contributor's local environment is darwin/arm64 without admin / sudo, which blocks the three obvious shapes of "real unsupported-hardlink mount": - `hdiutil create -fs exfat` fails with `operation not permitted` (system integrity protection blocks disk-image creation in this context). exfat would be the closest cheap match — on darwin, exfat rejects `link(2)` with `eopnotsupp`, which is one of the four codes the fallback's `hard_link_unsupported_codes` set covers. - `mount_smbfs` requires a reachable smb server (the reporter's mount class). not available locally. - fuse-based unsupported-link filesystems (9p / virtiofs / custom fuse) need `sudo mount` permissions. so the proof in the pr body intercepts at the same syscall boundary the real-mount error would surface from — `fs.promises.link` rejecting with `code: "enotsup"`, message prefix `enotsup: operation not supported on socket, link …` (verbatim from issue #81089's stack trace). the downstream `writefile(lockpath, payload, { flag: "wx" })` is real on-disk i/o against the actual darwin/arm64 `node:fs` runtime — apfs rather than the affected mount, but the contention semantics (`eexist` on second writer) are o_excl-level not filesystem-specific. so the only thing the monkey-patch substitutes for is the link-call rejection itself, which is exactly what the reporter's mount produces. two ways forward, picking whichever the team prefers: 1. **accept the existing proof under the maintainer-skip clause** in `agents.md` ("maintainers: may skip/ignore real behavior proof when local tests or crabbox verified behavior; record proof in pr verification."). the code shape was already accepted by the review summary ("the proposed code shape is the narrow maintainable fix"); only the proof origin is in question. 2. **run via crabbox / testbox with a real smb or 9p mount** for a true end-to-end. happy to update the pr body with the resulting log when a maintainer can trigger it — i cannot kick off crabbox myself. no further code push from my side until you say which path; the existing fix already matches the bot's accepted shape and the monkey-patch driver is the strongest reproducible proof under contributor control here.
commands.freeform_prompt: acknowledged on the real-environment gap — wanted to be transparent about why the PR body's proof stops where it does. This contributor's local environment is darwin/arm64 without admin / sudo, which blocks the three obvious shapes of "real unsupported-hardlink mount": - `hdiutil create -fs ExFAT` fails with `Operation not permitted` (System Integrity Protection blocks disk-image creation in this context). exFAT would be the closest cheap match — on darwin, exFAT rejects `link(2)` with `EOPNOTSUPP`, which is one of the four codes the fallback's `HARD_LINK_UNSUPPORTED_CODES` set covers. - `mount_smbfs` requires a reachable SMB server (the reporter's mount class). Not available locally. - FUSE-based unsupported-link filesystems (9p / virtiofs / custom FUSE) need `sudo mount` permissions. So the proof in the PR body intercepts at the same syscall boundary the real-mount error would surface from — `fs.promises.link` rejecting with `code: "ENOTSUP"`, message prefix `ENOTSUP: operation not supported on socket, link …` (verbatim from issue #81089's stack trace). The downstream `writeFile(lockPath, payload, { flag: "wx" })` is real on-disk I/O against the actual darwin/arm64 `node:fs` runtime — APFS rather than the affected mount, but the contention semantics (`EEXIST` on second writer) are O_EXCL-level not filesystem-specific. So the only thing the monkey-patch substitutes for is the link-call rejection itself, which is exactly what the reporter's mount produces. Two ways forward, picking whichever the team prefers: 1. **Accept the existing proof under the maintainer-skip clause** in `AGENTS.md` ("Maintainers: may skip/ignore Real behavior proof when local tests or Crabbox verified behavior; record proof in PR verification."). The code shape was already accepted by the review summary ("the proposed code shape is the narrow maintainable fix"); only the proof origin is in question. 2. **Run via Crabbox / Testbox with a real SMB or 9p mount** for a true end-to-end. Happy to update the PR body with the resulting log when a maintainer can trigger it — I cannot kick off Crabbox myself. No further code push from my side until you say which path; the existing fix already matches the bot's accepted shape and the monkey-patch driver is the strongest reproducible proof under contributor control here.

target.title: fix(session-cost-usage): fall back to O_EXCL write when fs.link is unsupported (#81089)
target.author: 0xghost42
